name: Alto del León and Puerto de Navacerrada Loop
name_original: Alto del León y Puerto de Navacerrada - Parque Nacional de la Sierra de Guadarrama — circular por el Embalse de Navacerrada
description: This difficult 59.6-mile road cycling route through Sierra de Guadarrama National Park features two major mountain passes and reservoir view
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Navas De Riofrío traverse a landscape characterized by significant elevation changes, including mountain passes and hilly terrain. The region features a mix of open roads and routes that pass by historical landmarks such as the Aqueduct of Segovia. Road cyclists can expect varied gradients, from sustained climbs to more rolling sections, offering diverse challenges. Best road cycling routes around Navas De Riofrío The most popular road cycling route is Alto del León and Puerto de Navacerrada Loop, a 59.6 miles (95.9 km) trail that takes 5 hours 8 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial climbs over mountain passes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Aqueduct of Segovia – Navafría Pass loop from Estación de Segovia, a difficult 61.3 miles (98.7 km) path. This route combines historical sights with challenging mountain pass ascents. Local road cyclists also love the Aqueduct of Segovia – View of Monte Atalaya loop from Estación de Segovia, a 37.8 miles (60.9 km) trail leading through historical areas and offering scenic viewpoints, often completed in about 2 hours 50 minutes. Road cycling around Navas De Riofrío is defined by challenging mountain passes, historical aqueducts, and varied hilly terrain. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Navas De Riofrío are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 2000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Navas De Riofrío's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Navas De Riofrío?
A: The region is characterized by significant elevation changes, including challenging mountain passes and varied hilly terrain. You'll encounter a mix of open roads and routes that pass by historical landmarks, offering diverse gradients from sustained climbs to more rolling sections.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: Yes, Navas De Riofrío offers a few easier road cycling routes. While many routes involve significant climbs, there are options with less demanding profiles. For example, the region has 3 routes classified as easy, and 37 as moderate, providing choices for different fitness levels.

Q: Which routes are recommended for experienced road cyclists seeking a challenge?
A: For experienced cyclists, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063924140/alto-del-leon-and-puerto-de-navacerrada-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del León and Puerto de Navacerrada Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer substantial climbs and cover nearly 96 km. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33653590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aqueduct of Segovia – Navafría Pass loop from Estación de Segovia&lt;/a&gt;, which combines historical sights with demanding mountain pass ascents over 98 km.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Navas De Riofrío are designed as loops. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063924140/alto-del-leon-and-puerto-de-navacerrada-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del León and Puerto de Navacerrada Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33653590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aqueduct of Segovia – Navafría Pass loop from Estación de Segovia&lt;/a&gt;, allowing you to start and finish at the same point.

Q: What historical landmarks or scenic viewpoints can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes often pass by significant historical sites and offer scenic views. You can encounter the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/778047" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aqueduct of Segovia&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy panoramic vistas from places like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3942583" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Canchas Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. Mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4225665" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navacerrada Pass&lt;/a&gt; are also prominent features.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Navas De Riofrío?
A: Given the region's mountain passes and elevation, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be hot, especially at lower elevations, while winter may bring snow to higher passes.

Q: Are there routes suitable for families with children?
A: While many routes feature significant climbs, some of the shorter, moderate options might be suitable for families with older, experienced children. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39539335" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roadbike loop from Estación de Segovia&lt;/a&gt; is a shorter 22.8 km route with less elevation gain, which could be a good starting point.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Navas De Riofrío?
A: The road cycling routes in Navas De Riofrío are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging mountain passes, the historical sights, and the varied hilly terrain that defines the region's cycling experience.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Navas De Riofrío area?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes available around Navas De Riofrío, catering to various skill levels from easy to difficult. This extensive network provides plenty of options for exploration.

Q: Are there any routes that combine historical sights with challenging climbs?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33653590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aqueduct of Segovia – Navafría Pass loop from Estación de Segovia&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example. This difficult 98.7 km route allows you to experience the historical grandeur of the Aqueduct of Segovia while tackling demanding ascents over mountain passes.

Q: What are some of the notable mountain passes encountered on these routes?
A: Many routes in the region feature significant mountain passes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4225665" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navacerrada Pass&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent feature on several challenging loops, offering both demanding climbs and rewarding views.
tips: The port of Guadarrama, which culminates in the Alto del León, is a Spanish mountain pass, which serves to cross the Sierra de Guadarrama (belonging to the Central system), connecting the province of Segovia and the Community of Madrid.

Guadarrama gives its name to the homonymous mountain range. It is located in the foothills and is a crossroads for cycling routes. From here you can climb the ports of León, Navacerrada or Cruz Verde, towards San Lorenzo de El Escorial.
Beautiful dam, especially when it is up to the top of the water, in possibly one of the most beautiful villages in Spain. Beautiful views!
Port of passage, with a length of about 18 kilometers, in stage 20 of the Tour of Spain 2015.

A natural space.
Stork nests on hundred-year-old ash trees.
Pasture forest.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this racebike route?
faqs.answer: This challenging racebike route, covering nearly 96 km with significant elevation changes, typically takes around 5 hours and 9 minutes to complete. This duration is for the cycling time itself and does not include stops.

faqs.question: Is this racebike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as 'difficult' and is not suitable for beginners. It features substantial climbs, including the Alto del León and Puerto de Navacerrada, requiring good physical fitness and experience with road cycling in mountainous terrain.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Alto del León y Puerto de Navacerrada racebike route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable points of interest. You'll pass the historic Alto del León Monument (Puerto de Guadarrama), enjoy the tranquil views of the Navacerrada Reservoir, and conquer the demanding Puerto de Navacerrada Ascent. The route also goes through the Revenga Grove and near the Main Square of Guadarrama.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect while cycling this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is highly varied and challenging. You'll encounter significant and sustained climbs on the mountain passes, such as the Alto del León and Puerto de Navacerrada. The section around the Embalse de Navacerrada offers a more rolling or flatter respite, typically on paved surfaces. Expect demanding ascents and descents throughout the mountainous sections.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Given that the route includes both Alto del León and Puerto de Navacerrada, common starting points with parking include areas near the Puerto de Navacerrada or in towns like Guadarrama or Navacerrada village. Specific parking lots can be found in these areas, often near the main roads leading to the passes.

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations or permits needed for cycling in the Parque Nacional de la Sierra de Guadarrama?
faqs.answer: Generally, road cycling on established public roads within the Parque Nacional de la Sierra de Guadarrama does not require special permits. However, it's crucial to stay on marked roads, respect speed limits, and be aware of any specific regulations regarding protected areas. Always check the official park website for the latest information on rules and any potential restrictions, especially for off-road cycling, which is often more regulated.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is generally more stable, and the mountain passes are clear of snow and ice. Winter cycling can be hazardous due to snow, ice, and colder temperatures, especially at higher elevations like Puerto de Navacerrada.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this racebike route?
faqs.answer: This is a racebike route primarily on public roads. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces and on leash, it's important to consider the nature of the activity. Cycling at speed with a dog can be unsafe for both the cyclist and the animal. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it is well-trained, on a leash, and that you can maintain control and safety throughout the ride, especially on busy roads.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or get refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, you will find options for food and refreshments. Both Alto del León (Puerto de Guadarrama) and Puerto de Navacerrada have establishments like restaurants and cafes. The towns around the Navacerrada Reservoir, such as Navacerrada village, also offer various services for cyclists.

faqs.question: Does this route follow any other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this racebike route incorporates sections of several other official trails. Notably, it follows parts of the [CIMA SE01] Navacerrada, Ruta del Sureste, [CIMA MA05] Bola del Mundo (Navacerrada) * Collado Villalba, and [CIMA MA09] El León. You'll also find smaller segments overlapping with the Ruta ADESGAM (Asociación de Desarrollo Sierra de Guadarrama) and the Madrid Way of Saint James.

faqs.question: Is this a circular route or an out-and-back?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route, meaning you will end your ride at or near your starting point without having to retrace your path. This allows for a varied and continuously changing landscape throughout the journey.
